content.full_text:
November 2, 1976

Gilbert B. Perry
7367 Viewpoint Rd.
Aptos, CA  95003

Dear Mr. Perry:

     This is in response to your letter of October 25, 1976, regarding the filing requirements of retired judges. Please be advised that as a judge sitting on assignment, you are not required to file the Statement of Economic Interests.

     If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact this office.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Dawn Wiser
                                        Technical Assistance and
                                        Analysis Division

CHAMBERS OF

The Superior Court

SANTA CRUZ, CALIFORNIA

GILBERT B. PERRY (RET.)
JUDGE

Fair Employment Practises Comm
Technical Assistance Section
Sacramento, Calif 95804

Gentlemen:

I retired Jan. 1 1975 and have assisted
superior courts from time to time since then —
I have not been able to fully understand the
applicability of the "settlement of economic interest" as it
applies to retired judge —
Will you be kind enough to advise me if your
requirement and provide me any forms that be
necessary —

Very truly Yours
Gilbert B. Perry

GILBERT B. PERRY
7367 VIEWPOINT RD.
APTOS, CAL.
95003

sections.question: I retired Jan. 1st 1975 and have assisted Superior Courts from time to time since then — I have not been able to fully understand the application of the "Statement of Economic Interests" as it applies to retired judges — Will you be kind enough to advise me of your requirements, and provide me any forms that are necessary —
sections.conclusion: Please be advised that as a judge sitting on assignment, you are not required to file the Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.question_synthetic: A retired judge who has provided occasional assistance to Superior Courts inquires whether retired judges are required to file a Statement of Economic Interests and requests information on applicable filing requirements and forms.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Retired judges serving on assignment are not required to file the Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains a clear Q/A structure with incoming request letter followed by FPPC response letter dated November 2, 1976. The document appears to have some quality/OCR issues with a second fragmented letter visible, but the primary response is clear and legible. Minor discrepancy: recipient name appears as both 'Perry' and 'Berry' in different sections.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: I retired Jan. 1st 1975 and have assisted Superior Courts from time to time since then — I have not been able to fully understand the application of the "Statement of Economic Interests" as it applies to retired judges — Will you be kind enough to advise me of your requirements, and provide me any forms that are necessary —

CONCLUSION: Please be advised that as a judge sitting on assignment, you are not required to file the Statement of Economic Interests.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to a retired judge's inquiry regarding Statement of Economic Interests filing requirements, advising that judges on assignment are exempt from this filing requirement.
